title: The Money Question You've Been Avoiding
subtitle: A journal prompt set for finding the thing you haven't let yourself look at yet
excerpt: Five journal prompts for locating the one money question you keep not asking yourself. Useful whether you have a vague, unnamed money unease or a specific thing you know about and haven't written down yet.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education as part of the beginner entry points for money work.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you feel a low-level money unease you haven't named, or when you know what the stuck point is and have been avoiding writing about it.

content.text: Five prompts for finding the money question you haven't let yourself ask yet. Some people arrive here with a clearly named thing they've been avoiding. Others arrive with a formless unease and no idea where it centres. Both work here.
Write for ten minutes total. Spend two minutes on each warm-up prompt, then two to three minutes on each closer-in prompt. Don't stop to edit.
When I think about money this week, what comes up first?
Is there a money topic I find myself changing the subject on, in my head or out loud?
If I had to name the one money question I keep not asking myself, what would it be?
What am I afraid the answer might be?
What would change if I let myself look at this directly?
Written for homemade.education as part of the beginner entry points for money work.
